verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (hK2) is a secreted serine protease of the kallikrein family. It is synthesized as a preproprotein, processed, and secreted into the extracellular milieu — primarily into seminal plasma and the prostate tissue environment. It has no transmembrane domain, no GPI anchor, and no documented stable covalent or wash-resistant tethering to a cell-surface partner. Its presence outside the cell is as a soluble secreted enzyme, not as a stable surface-anchored protein. There are no antibody/ADC/CAR-T programs targeting it on the cell surface; it is tracked as a serum biomarker. Verdict: secreted only.
